message:
feat: initial nodemode package — Node.js primitives on Cloudflare

Map fs to R2 + DO SQLite, child_process to built-in command emulators
+ Containers, stdio to WebSocket, and process to DO instance state.

Includes:
- FsEngine: R2-backed filesystem with SQLite directory index and cache
- ProcessManager: 24 built-in shell command emulators with tiered execution
- Workspace: Durable Object coordinator with WebSocket hibernation API
- HTTP handler with REST API for all fs and process operations
- CLI tool (npx nodemode init/deploy)
- 17 integration tests via @cloudflare/vitest-pool-workers
- Astro Starlight documentation site (8 pages)
- Architecture diagram (SVG + Excalidraw source)
- Research notes on feasibility and edgebox code reuse

@@ -0,0 +1,54 @@
+# nodemode
+
+Map Node.js primitives to Cloudflare Workers + R2 + Durable Objects + Containers.
+
+## Mapping
+
+| Node.js | Cloudflare |
+|---------|------------|
+| `fs` | R2 (file content) + DO SQLite (directory index & cache) |
+| `child_process` | DO built-in commands + Containers for heavy workloads |
+| `stdio` | WebSocket with DO SQLite persistence |
+| `process` | DO instance state |
+
+## Quick Start
+
+```bash
+npx nodemode init my-workspace
+cd my-workspace
+npx wrangler dev
+```
+
+## Architecture
+
+```
+Client ──WebSocket──▶ Worker ──▶ Durable Object (Workspace)
+                                   ├── SQLite (metadata, process table, cache)
+                                   ├── R2 (file content)
+                                   └── Container (npm, node, heavy commands)
+```
+
+Commands are tiered:
+- **Built-in** (cat, ls, grep, echo, pwd, ...) — execute directly in DO, $0 cost, <1ms
+- **Container** (npm, node, git, ...) — spawn Cloudflare Container, ~$0.02/hr
+
+## Storage
+
+- **R2** — unlimited file storage keyed by `{workspace_id}/{path}`
+- **DO SQLite** — directory index, file cache (<64KB), process table, terminal buffer
+
+## Deploy
+
+```bash
+npx nodemode deploy
+```
+
+Requires Cloudflare Workers Paid plan (for Durable Objects + R2).
+
+## Docs
+
+See the [documentation site](docs/) built with Astro Starlight.
+
+## License
+
+MIT

@@ -0,0 +1,24 @@
+{
+  "$schema": "node_modules/wrangler/config-schema.json",
+  "name": "my-nodemode-workspace",
+  "main": "./worker/index.ts",
+  "compatibility_date": "2025-12-01",
+  "compatibility_flags": ["nodejs_compat"],
+
+  "r2_buckets": [
+    {
+      "binding": "FS_BUCKET",
+      "bucket_name": "nodemode-fs"
+    }
+  ],
+
+  "durable_objects": {
+    "bindings": [
+      { "name": "WORKSPACE", "class_name": "Workspace" }
+    ]
+  },
+
+  "migrations": [
+    { "tag": "v1", "new_sqlite_classes": ["Workspace"] }
+  ]
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,75 @@ +--- +title: Architecture +description: How nodemode maps Node.js to Cloudflare +--- + +## System Overview + + + +## Tiered Command Execution + +``` +command arrives at Workspace DO + │ + ▼ + Is it a built-in? (cat, ls, grep, echo, pwd, ...) + │ + ├── YES → Execute in DO directly + │ $0 cost, <1ms latency + │ Reads/writes via FsEngine (R2 + SQLite) + │ + └── NO → Spawn Cloudflare Container + ~$0.02/hr, real Linux filesystem + getTcpPort().fetch() for communication + Container runs command → returns result → sleeps +``` + +## Storage Strategy + +### R2 for File Content + +Files are stored as R2 objects keyed by `{workspace_id}/{path}`. R2 provides: +- Unlimited storage ($0.015/GB/month) +- Streaming reads/writes (128MB Worker memory limit safe) +- Global distribution + +### DO SQLite for Metadata + +R2 is a flat key-value store — it has no directory concept. DO SQLite provides: +- Directory index (path → size, mode, mtime, is_dir) +- Hot file cache (files < 64KB cached in SQLite for <1ms reads vs R2's 10-50ms) +- Process table (command history, stdout/stderr capture) +- Terminal buffer (persists output across DO hibernation) + +### Why This Split? + +| Concern | R2 | DO SQLite | +|---------|----|-----------| +| File content | Cheap, unlimited, streaming | 10GB limit, no streaming | +| Directory listing | Slow (`list()` with prefix) | Fast (<1ms SQL query) | +| File metadata | HEAD request (~10ms) | Instant (<1ms) | +| Consistency | Eventually consistent | Strongly consistent | +| Hot reads | 10-50ms | <1ms (cached) | + +## Workspace Isolation + +Each workspace is a separate Durable Object instance: +- Own SQLite database (filesystem index, process table) +- Own R2 prefix (`{workspace_id}/`) +- Own WebSocket connections +- Single-threaded (no locks needed) +- Hibernates when idle (zero cost) + +## Composition with Other *mode Packages + +``` +termweb workspace +├── gitmode — git storage (R2 + DO SQLite) +├── nodemode — Node.js runtime (R2 fs + DO child_process + WebSocket stdio) +├── pymode — Python runtime (CPython WASM + DO fan-out) +├── querymode — SQL queries (columnar engine + R2 spill) +└── workspace — orchestrator (auth + routing + lifecycle) +``` + +Each package is independent and composable via service bindings or direct import.

+---
+title: Child Process
+description: Tiered command execution — built-in emulators + Containers
+---
+
+## Overview
+
+nodemode's `ProcessManager` maps `child_process.exec()` to a tiered execution model:
+
+1. **Built-in emulators** — common commands run directly in the DO
+2. **Cloudflare Containers** — heavy commands run in real Linux containers
+
+## Built-in Commands
+
+These execute in the DO with zero cost and sub-millisecond latency:
+
+| Command | Description |
+|---------|------------|
+| `echo` | Print arguments |
+| `cat` | Read file contents |
+| `ls` / `ls -l` / `ls -a` | List directory |
+| `pwd` | Print working directory |
+| `env` | Print environment variables |
+| `head` / `tail` | First/last N lines |
+| `wc` | Word/line/byte count |
+| `grep` | Pattern search in files |
+| `mkdir` / `mkdir -p` | Create directories |
+| `rm` / `rm -rf` | Remove files/directories |
+| `cp` | Copy files |
+| `mv` | Move/rename files |
+| `touch` | Create empty file |
+| `basename` / `dirname` | Path manipulation |
+| `which` | Locate command |
+| `whoami` | Current user |
+| `date` | Current date/time |
+| `test` | File/string tests (`-f`, `-d`, `-e`, `-z`, `-n`) |
+| `true` / `false` | Exit 0 / exit 1 |
+| `printf` | Formatted output |
+
+## REST API
+
+### Execute a command
+
+```
+POST /workspace/{id}/exec
+Content-Type: application/json
+
+{"command": "cat src/index.ts", "cwd": "/project"}
+```
+
+Response:
+```json
+{
+  "exitCode": 0,
+  "stdout": "console.log('hello');\n",
+  "stderr": ""
+}
+```
+
+### List processes
+
+```
+GET /workspace/{id}/process/list
+```
+
+### Get process details
+
+```
+GET /workspace/{id}/process/get?pid=42
+```
+
+## Command Parsing
+
+Commands are parsed with basic shell-like quoting:
+
+```bash
+echo "hello world"        → echo ["hello world"]
+cat 'file name.txt'       → cat ["file name.txt"]
+grep pattern file.txt     → grep ["pattern", "file.txt"]
+```
+
+## Container Execution (Planned)
+
+For commands not in the built-in set (npm, node, python, cargo, etc.), nodemode will spawn a Cloudflare Container:
+
+```
+Workspace DO receives "npm install"
+    → Not a built-in command
+    → Spawn Container with Node.js image
+    → getTcpPort(8080).fetch() to send command
+    → Container executes in real Linux
+    → Returns stdout/stderr/exitCode
+    → Container sleeps (billed only while active)
+```
+
+This mirrors the edgebox architecture where commands are tiered between WASM-native execution and OS-level process spawning.

+---
+title: Deployment
+description: Deploy nodemode to Cloudflare Workers
+---
+
+## One-Command Deploy
+
+```bash
+npx nodemode deploy
+```
+
+This runs `npx wrangler deploy` under the hood.
+
+## Prerequisites
+
+1. A Cloudflare account with Workers Paid plan (for Durable Objects + R2)
+2. `wrangler` authenticated: `npx wrangler login`
+
+## What Gets Deployed
+
+- **Worker** — stateless HTTP router (`worker/index.ts`)
+- **Durable Object** — `Workspace` class with SQLite storage
+- **R2 Bucket** — `nodemode-fs` for file content
+
+## Resource Limits
+
+| Resource | Limit |
+|----------|-------|
+| Worker memory | 128 MB per isolate |
+| DO SQLite storage | 10 GB per DO (paid plan) |
+| R2 storage | Unlimited ($0.015/GB/month) |
+| DO CPU per invocation | 30s (configurable via `limits.cpu_ms`) |
+| R2 read latency | 10-50ms (cache hot files in SQLite) |
+| DO SQLite read latency | <1ms |
+
+## Custom Domain
+
+Add a custom domain in the Cloudflare dashboard or via wrangler:
+
+```jsonc
+// wrangler.jsonc
+{
+  "routes": [
+    { "pattern": "nodemode.example.com/*", "zone_name": "example.com" }
+  ]
+}
+```
+
+## Environment Variables
+
+Set secrets via wrangler:
+
+```bash
+npx wrangler secret put API_KEY
+```
+
+Access in your handler via `env.API_KEY`.
+
+## Scaling
+
+nodemode scales automatically:
+- **Workers** — thousands of concurrent requests, globally distributed
+- **Durable Objects** — one per workspace, single-threaded, hibernates when idle
+- **R2** — unlimited storage, global replication
+
+Each workspace is isolated. Creating 10,000 workspaces means 10,000 independent DOs, each with their own SQLite database and R2 prefix.

+---
+title: Filesystem (fs)
+description: R2-backed filesystem with SQLite directory index
+---
+
+## Overview
+
+nodemode's `FsEngine` maps Node.js `fs` operations to R2 (file content) + DO SQLite (directory index and cache).
+
+## Supported Operations
+
+| Node.js fs | nodemode | Backing |
+|-----------|----------|---------|
+| `readFile` | `fs.readFile(path)` | SQLite cache → R2 fallback |
+| `writeFile` | `fs.writeFile(path, data)` | R2 put + SQLite index |
+| `appendFile` | `fs.appendFile(path, data)` | Read + concat + write |
+| `unlink` | `fs.unlink(path)` | R2 delete + SQLite delete |
+| `stat` | `fs.stat(path)` | SQLite lookup |
+| `exists` | `fs.exists(path)` | SQLite lookup |
+| `readdir` | `fs.readdir(path)` | SQLite prefix query |
+| `mkdir` | `fs.mkdir(path)` | SQLite directory marker |
+| `rmdir` | `fs.rmdir(path, recursive?)` | R2 batch delete + SQLite |
+| `rename` | `fs.rename(old, new)` | R2 copy+delete + SQLite |
+| `chmod` | `fs.chmod(path, mode)` | SQLite update |
+
+## REST API
+
+### Write a file
+
+```
+POST /workspace/{id}/fs/write
+Content-Type: application/json
+
+{"path": "src/index.ts", "content": "console.log('hello');", "mode": 420}
+```
+
+### Read a file
+
+```
+POST /workspace/{id}/fs/read
+Content-Type: application/json
+
+{"path": "src/index.ts"}
+```
+
+### Stat a file
+
+```
+GET /workspace/{id}/fs/stat?path=src/index.ts
+```
+
+### List directory
+
+```
+GET /workspace/{id}/fs/readdir?path=src
+```
+
+### Create directory
+
+```
+POST /workspace/{id}/fs/mkdir
+Content-Type: application/json
+
+{"path": "src/lib", "recursive": true}
+```
+
+### Delete a file
+
+```
+POST /workspace/{id}/fs/unlink
+Content-Type: application/json
+
+{"path": "src/old.ts"}
+```
+
+### Rename a file
+
+```
+POST /workspace/{id}/fs/rename
+Content-Type: application/json
+
+{"oldPath": "src/old.ts", "newPath": "src/new.ts"}
+```
+
+### Check existence
+
+```
+GET /workspace/{id}/fs/exists?path=src/index.ts
+```
+
+## Caching Strategy
+
+Files smaller than 64KB are cached in DO SQLite after the first R2 read. Subsequent reads hit SQLite (<1ms) instead of R2 (10-50ms).
+
+Cache is invalidated on write (updated) or delete (evicted).
+
+## R2 Key Structure
+
+```
+{workspace_id}/
+├── src/
+│   ├── index.ts      → R2 key: "{workspace_id}/src/index.ts"
+│   └── lib/
+│       └── util.ts   → R2 key: "{workspace_id}/src/lib/util.ts"
+├── package.json      → R2 key: "{workspace_id}/package.json"
+└── .gitignore        → R2 key: "{workspace_id}/.gitignore"
+```
+
+R2 is flat key-value — the directory structure is maintained entirely in SQLite. The `files` table stores path, size, mode, mtime, and an `is_dir` flag.

+---
+title: Getting Started
+description: Set up nodemode in under 5 minutes
+---
+
+## Prerequisites
+
+- A [Cloudflare account](https://dash.cloudflare.com/sign-up)
+- Node.js 18+ and npm/pnpm
+
+## Option 1: CLI Scaffold
+
+```bash
+mkdir my-workspace && cd my-workspace
+npx nodemode init
+npm install
+npx nodemode deploy
+```
+
+This creates:
+- `worker/index.ts` — Worker entry point
+- `wrangler.jsonc` — Cloudflare config (R2 bucket + DO bindings)
+- `package.json` — with nodemode dependency
+
+## Option 2: Manual Setup
+
+```bash
+npm init -y
+npm install nodemode
+npm install -D wrangler @cloudflare/workers-types typescript
+```
+
+Create `worker/index.ts`:
+
+```ts
+import { Workspace, createHandler } from "nodemode";
+import type { Env } from "nodemode";
+
+export { Workspace };
+
+const handler = createHandler();
+
+export default {
+  async fetch(request: Request, env: Env): Promise<Response> {
+    return handler(request, env);
+  },
+};
+```
+
+Create `wrangler.jsonc`:
+
+```jsonc
+{
+  "name": "my-workspace",
+  "main": "./worker/index.ts",
+  "compatibility_date": "2025-12-01",
+  "compatibility_flags": ["nodejs_compat"],
+
+  "r2_buckets": [
+    { "binding": "FS_BUCKET", "bucket_name": "nodemode-fs" }
+  ],
+
+  "durable_objects": {
+    "bindings": [
+      { "name": "WORKSPACE", "class_name": "Workspace" }
+    ]
+  },
+
+  "migrations": [
+    { "tag": "v1", "new_sqlite_classes": ["Workspace"] }
+  ]
+}
+```
+
+Deploy:
+
+```bash
+npx wrangler deploy
+```
+
+## Using the API
+
+Once deployed, interact with your workspace via HTTP:
+
+```bash
+BASE="https://my-workspace.your-subdomain.workers.dev"
+WS="my-project"
+
+# Initialize workspace
+curl -X POST "$BASE/workspace/$WS/init" \
+  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
+  -d '{"owner": "alice", "name": "my-project"}'
+
+# Write a file
+curl -X POST "$BASE/workspace/$WS/fs/write" \
+  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
+  -d '{"path": "hello.txt", "content": "Hello from nodemode!"}'
+
+# Read a file
+curl -X POST "$BASE/workspace/$WS/fs/read" \
+  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
+  -d '{"path": "hello.txt"}'
+
+# Run a command
+curl -X POST "$BASE/workspace/$WS/exec" \
+  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
+  -d '{"command": "cat hello.txt"}'
+
+# List directory
+curl "$BASE/workspace/$WS/fs/readdir?path=/"
+```
+
+## Local Development
+
+```bash
+npx wrangler dev
+```
+
+This starts a local development server with Miniflare, giving you a local R2 bucket and Durable Objects for testing.

+---
+title: nodemode
+description: Node.js runtime on Cloudflare Workers — R2 filesystem, DO child_process, Container execution
+---
+
+**nodemode** maps Node.js primitives to Cloudflare primitives, giving you a serverless Node.js environment without VMs.
+
+## Mapping
+
+| Node.js | Cloudflare Primitive | Status |
+|---------|---------------------|--------|
+| `fs.readFile` / `writeFile` | R2 `get()` / `put()` | Works today |
+| `fs.readdir` | R2 `list()` + DO SQLite index | Works today |
+| `fs.stat` | DO SQLite metadata | Works today |
+| `child_process.exec` (light) | Built-in emulators in DO | Works today |
+| `child_process.spawn` (heavy) | Cloudflare Container | Planned |
+| `stdin` / `stdout` | WebSocket to browser | Works today |
+| `stdout` persistence | DO SQLite buffer | Works today |
+| `process.env` | Worker env / Secrets | Works today |
+| `os.tmpdir` / temp files | DO SQLite | Works today |
+
+## Quick Start
+
+```bash
+npx nodemode init
+npx nodemode deploy
+```
+
+```ts
+import { Workspace, createHandler } from "nodemode";
+
+export { Workspace };
+export default { fetch: createHandler() };
+```
+
+## Architecture
+
+nodemode runs as a Cloudflare Worker with two backing services:
+
+- **R2** — virtual filesystem (file content as R2 objects)
+- **Durable Objects + SQLite** — per-workspace state (directory index, process table, terminal buffer)
+
+Each workspace is an isolated DO instance with its own filesystem and process table. Commands execute in a tiered model:
+
+1. **Built-in** (cat, ls, grep, echo) — handled in DO, zero cost, sub-millisecond
+2. **Container** (npm, node, python) — spawns Cloudflare Container, real Linux
+
+## Related Projects
+
+- [gitmode](https://github.com/teamchong/gitmode) — Git server on Cloudflare Workers
+- [pymode](https://github.com/teamchong/pymode) — Python runtime on Cloudflare Workers
+- [querymode](https://github.com/teamchong/querymode) — Columnar query engine on Cloudflare Workers

+---
+title: WebSocket stdio
+description: Stream stdin/stdout/stderr over WebSocket with hibernation persistence
+---
+
+## Overview
+
+nodemode provides real-time terminal I/O via WebSocket, with output persistence across DO hibernation using SQLite buffering.
+
+## Connecting
+
+Upgrade any workspace endpoint to WebSocket:
+
+```javascript
+const ws = new WebSocket("wss://my-workspace.example.com/workspace/my-project/");
+
+ws.onmessage = (event) => {
+  const msg = JSON.parse(event.data);
+  // msg.stream: "stdout" | "stderr"
+  // msg.data: string content
+  console.log(`[${msg.stream}] ${msg.data}`);
+};
+
+// Execute a command
+ws.send(JSON.stringify({
+  type: "exec",
+  command: "ls -la",
+}));
+```
+
+## Message Format
+
+### Client → Server
+
+```json
+{
+  "type": "exec",
+  "command": "grep TODO src/*.ts",
+  "cwd": "/project"
+}
+```
+
+### Server → Client (result)
+
+```json
+{
+  "type": "result",
+  "exitCode": 0,
+  "stdout": "src/index.ts:42: // TODO fix this\n",
+  "stderr": ""
+}
+```
+
+### Server → Client (broadcast)
+
+When multiple clients are connected, stdout/stderr from one client's commands are broadcast to all others:
+
+```json
+{
+  "stream": "stdout",
+  "data": "src/index.ts:42: // TODO fix this\n"
+}
+```
+
+## Hibernation Persistence
+
+When a DO hibernates (after ~10s of inactivity), the WebSocket is torn down. nodemode persists terminal output in SQLite's `terminal_buffer` table.
+
+When a new WebSocket connects, it receives the buffered output history before any new commands.
+
+This means:
+1. Run a long command
+2. Close your browser
+3. Reopen — see the output that happened while disconnected
+
+## Integration with xterm.js
+
+```javascript
+import { Terminal } from "xterm";
+
+const term = new Terminal();
+term.open(document.getElementById("terminal"));
+
+const ws = new WebSocket("wss://...");
+
+ws.onmessage = (event) => {
+  const msg = JSON.parse(event.data);
+  if (msg.stream === "stdout" || msg.type === "result") {
+    term.write(msg.data || msg.stdout || "");
+  }
+  if (msg.stream === "stderr" || msg.stderr) {
+    term.write(`\x1b[31m${msg.stderr || msg.data}\x1b[0m`);
+  }
+};
+
+term.onData((data) => {
+  ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: "exec", command: data.trim() }));
+});
+```

+# Edgebox Code Reuse Analysis
+
+## Key files in edgebox for nodemode
+
+### High Priority — Direct Reuse
+
+| File | Size | What | nodemode adaptation |
+|------|------|------|---------------------|
+| `polyfills/modules/child_process.js` | 3KB | ChildProcess EventEmitter, stdin/stdout/stderr | Swap WAMR host calls for DO RPC / Container fetch |
+| `wasi_process.zig` | 12KB | JSON command allowlist with subcommand filtering | Port to TS (~50 lines) |
+| `polyfills/modules/events.js` | 2KB | EventEmitter (on, emit, once, removeListener) | Direct reuse |
+| `polyfills/modules/path.js` | — | path.join, resolve, parse, basename, dirname | Direct reuse (pure JS) |
+| `polyfills/modules/os.js` | — | platform, arch, tmpdir, homedir | Direct reuse (static values) |
+| `polyfills/modules/util.js` | — | format, promisify, inspect | Direct reuse (pure JS) |
+
+### Medium Priority — Concept Reuse
+
+| File | What | Adaptation |
+|------|------|------------|
+| `polyfills/fs.zig` | Full fs API surface (86KB) | Use as API spec — implement against R2 + SQLite |
+| `polyfills/require.zig` | CommonJS module resolution | Port to TS — resolve against R2 keys |
+| `edgebox_wamr.zig` | Module caching (daemon mode) | Cache compiled modules in R2 |
+| `wasi_tty.zig` | TTY detection, ANSI codes | Use for interactive terminal mode |
+
+### Lower Priority — Reference Only
+
+| File | What | Notes |
+|------|------|-------|
+| `polyfills/crypto.zig` | TLS 1.3, HMAC, SHA256 | WebCrypto already available |
+| `polyfills/net.zig` | Network shims | Service bindings handle networking |
+| `freeze/` | Comptime bytecode-to-C | Workers do pre-compilation already |
+
+## Permission System (port target)
+
+edgebox's `.edgebox.json`:
+```json
+{
+  "allowCommands": ["git", "npm", "node"],
+  "denyCommands": ["sudo", "su", "rm"],
+  "commands": {
+    "git": ["clone", "status", "add", "commit"],
+    "npm": true,
+    "node": true
+  }
+}
+```
+
+nodemode equivalent — same format, enforced in ProcessManager before execution.
+
+## Key Insight
+
+edgebox maps: `Node.js APIs → WASI syscalls → host functions → OS`
+nodemode maps: `Node.js APIs → R2/DO SQLite → Cloudflare primitives`
+
+The API surface is identical. We're swapping the backend, not the interface.

+# nodemode Feasibility Research
+
+## Conclusion: Very Feasible
+
+All required Cloudflare primitives are GA as of March 2026. The hardest work (R2-as-fs, DO-as-process) is already proven across gitmode, pymode, and edgebox.
+
+## Cloudflare Primitives (March 2026)
+
+| Primitive | Status | Key Specs |
+|-----------|--------|-----------|
+| Containers | GA (June 2025) | Real Linux, writable fs, `getTcpPort().fetch()` for DO↔Container |
+| DO SQLite | GA | 10GB/DO (paid), strongly consistent, <1ms reads |
+| R2 | GA | Streaming read/write, ~10-50ms latency, occasional 400ms cold spikes |
+| nodejs_compat | GA | crypto, Buffer, streams, util, path, events — NOT fs, child_process, net |
+| DO WebSocket | GA | Hibernation API, automatic reconnection |
+| Service bindings | GA | Zero-cost Worker↔Worker RPC, WorkerEntrypoint class |
+| Worker memory | 128MB per isolate (same for DOs) |
+
+## Node.js → Cloudflare Mapping
+
+| Node.js | Cloudflare | Proven By |
+|---------|-----------|-----------|
+| fs.readFile/writeFile | R2 get/put | gitmode (worktree materialization) |
+| fs.readdir | R2 list + DO SQLite index | gitmode (listFiles) |
+| fs.stat | DO SQLite metadata | gitmode (file_sizes table) |
+| child_process.spawn (heavy) | Container | Cloudflare docs (getTcpPort) |
+| child_process.spawn (light) | DO RPC | pymode (thread_spawn → child DO) |
+| stdin/stdout | WebSocket + DO SQLite buffer | Standard DO WebSocket pattern |
+| process.env | Worker env / Secrets | Native |
+| os.tmpdir | DO SQLite | Trivial |
+| crypto | Web Crypto API | Native (nodejs_compat) |
+| net.Socket | Service bindings / DO WebSocket | Native |
+
+## Reusable Code from Existing Repos
+
+### gitmode
+- R2 storage patterns (chunk bundling, SQLite index)
+- Worktree materialization (incremental, batched)
+- Package structure (exports, CLI, wrangler config)
+
+### pymode
+- DO fan-out as threads (spawn → child DO, pickle args, join result)
+- Host imports pattern (WASM ↔ JS bridge)
+- Asyncify pattern for blocking operations
+
+### edgebox
+- Node.js API surface (58 compat tests)
+- Built-in command emulators (cat, ls, grep, echo)
+- Permission system (JSON command allowlist)
+- EventEmitter, ChildProcess class
+- Shell command parsing
+
+### querymode
+- R2 spill backend for memory-bounded operations
+- DO-based query execution pattern
+
+## What's Hard
+
+1. **R2 latency** — 10-50ms vs <1ms disk. Mitigate with DO SQLite cache for hot files.
+2. **Directory semantics** — R2 is flat. Need SQLite directory index. Tedious but solvable.
+3. **Claude CLI in DO** — 50MB JS + 128MB limit. Must run in Container, DO coordinates.
+4. **Streaming I/O** — `cmd1 | cmd2` needs Container↔Container pipes. Complex but doable with WebSocket.
+
+## What's Easy
+
+- File read/write (gitmode proves R2 + DO works)
+- Terminal output (WebSocket + SQLite buffer)
+- LLM API calls (fetch() is native)
+- Process isolation (each workspace is its own DO)
+- Built-in command emulators (pure TypeScript)

+-- nodemode per-workspace DO SQLite schema
+-- Each Workspace Durable Object instance creates these tables
+-- in its embedded SQLite database on first access.
+
+-- Filesystem index (R2 is flat key-value, this gives us directory semantics)
+CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS files (
+  path TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
+  r2_key TEXT NOT NULL,
+  size INTEGER NOT NULL,
+  mode INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0o644,
+  mtime INTEGER NOT NULL,
+  is_dir INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0
+);
+
+CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_files_parent ON files(
+  substr(path, 1, length(path) - length(replace(path, '/', '')) - length(substr(path, length(path) - instr(reverse(path), '/') + 2)))
+);
+
+-- File content cache (hot files cached in SQLite for <1ms reads)
+CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS file_cache (
+  path TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
+  data BLOB NOT NULL,
+  cached_at INTEGER NOT NULL
+);
+
+-- Process state
+CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS processes (
+  pid INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
+  command TEXT NOT NULL,
+  args TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',
+  status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'running',
+  exit_code INTEGER,
+  stdout TEXT,
+  stderr TEXT,
+  created_at INTEGER NOT NULL,
+  finished_at INTEGER
+);
+
+-- Workspace metadata
+CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspace_meta (
+  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY CHECK (id = 1),
+  owner TEXT NOT NULL,
+  name TEXT NOT NULL,
+  cwd TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '/',
+  env TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
+  created_at TEXT NOT NULL
+);
+
+-- Terminal output buffer (persists across DO hibernation)
+CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS terminal_buffer (
+  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
+  stream TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (stream IN ('stdout', 'stderr', 'stdin')),
+  data TEXT NOT NULL,
+  timestamp INTEGER NOT NULL
+);

+  "$schema": "node_modules/wrangler/config-schema.json",
+  "name": "nodemode",
+  "main": "./worker/index.ts",
+  "compatibility_date": "2025-12-01",
+  "compatibility_flags": ["nodejs_compat"],
+
+  // R2: virtual filesystem storage
+  "r2_buckets": [
+    {
+      "binding": "FS_BUCKET",
+      "bucket_name": "nodemode-fs"
+    }
+  ],
+
+  // Durable Objects: per-workspace process manager + filesystem index
+  "durable_objects": {
+    "bindings": [
+      { "name": "WORKSPACE", "class_name": "Workspace" }
+    ]
+  },
+
+  "migrations": [
+    { "tag": "v1", "new_sqlite_classes": ["Workspace"] }
+  ]
+}
